surface.name: Docusign eSignature REST API main Node.js Quickstart first run
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, the official main Node.js Quickstart is the fastest commonly used documented route: it downloads a personalized, preconfigured project and documents its first-run destination. Developer signup is one opaque step at the official signup URL; the public post-login generator and local run steps are reconstructed from official docs.

alternatives_considered.name: Authorization Code Grant embedded signing Quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This smaller specialized project includes one embedded-signing workflow, while the overview labels Multiple code examples as the main Quickstart and documents its first-run behavior.

alternatives_considered.name: JWT Grant remote signing Quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This smaller specialized project includes one remote-signing workflow and uses JWT, while the main project's documented first run uses Authorization Code Grant and opens embedded signing.

alternatives_considered.name: Manually configured Node.js code launcher
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The launcher exposes the same broad code examples but requires manual account configuration. The main Quickstart ZIP is preconfigured and has a documented first-run example.

entry_point.developer_intent: Start without a Docusign account, create the free developer sandbox account, generate the main eSignature REST API Node.js Quickstart, run its preconfigured project locally, authenticate through its documented first-run Authorization Code Grant route, and open the embedded-signing example.
assumed_prior_state: No Docusign account, developer sandbox, app, integration key, secret, RSA key, user GUID, redirect URI, consent, Quickstart ZIP, Node.js environment, signer identity, or envelope is assumed.
A supported computer, browser, terminal, npm environment, email address, and a signer name and email are available.

No account form was submitted, no verification or login was completed, and no app, integration key, secret, RSA key, consent, Quickstart, package installation, local server, or signing request was created during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: On the first run of the main Quickstart project, authenticate with Authorization Code Grant and open the embedded-signing example.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The intended first success is the preconfigured main Node.js Quickstart running locally, authenticating with Authorization Code Grant, and opening its embedded-signing code example.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The local Quickstart opens the embedded-signing example after authentication, which the official overview identifies as its first-run demonstration of basic Docusign functionality; the docs do not identify a later completed-envelope response as part of this default terminal.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: On first run, authenticate with Authorization Code Grant and open the embedded-signing example.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The official overview labels Multiple code examples as the main Quickstart and directly states what its first run opens. A completed signature or envelope response would go beyond that published terminal.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a free Docusign developer account for the sandbox environment and be its owner or account admin, because Quickstart creates a new app and integration key.

Name the app, select Node.js, and choose the main Multiple code examples, Authorization Code Grant and JWT Grant project; Quickstart creates and preconfigures its app, integration key, account settings, and authentication code.

For a manually configured launcher instead of the selected Quickstart, provide an integration key and secret for Authorization Code Grant, or an integration key, RSA key pair, and impersonated user's User ID GUID for JWT Grant.
For a manually configured local launcher, register `http://localhost:3000/ds/callback` as the redirect URI.
Have Node.js 8.10 or later and npm 5 or later according to the official repository's stated prerequisite.
For manual launcher configuration, provide the signer name and email in `appsettings.json`.

primary_path.action: Open the eSignature REST API Quickstart and overview, then identify the main Node.js project and its documented first-run behavior.
details: The generator requires an account owner or admin because it creates a new app and integration key.
The overview calls Multiple code examples, Authorization Code Grant and JWT Grant the main project; its first run authenticates with Authorization Code Grant and opens the embedded-signing example.
Smaller Authorization Code Grant embedded-signing and JWT remote-signing projects remain selectable alternatives.
primary_path.output: The main Quickstart surface, generation choices, prerequisites, run commands, and first-run terminal are identified.
primary_path.success_signal: The official overview enumerates login, app naming, language and project selection, ZIP download, extraction and run, plus the main project's first-run destination.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The generator itself requires an authenticated eligible developer account.

primary_path.action: Sign up for the free Docusign developer account.
details: Treat signup as one opaque workshop-policy step; use only the official signup URL.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer can access the Quickstart generator as the account owner or admin.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use the account recovery and verification instructions Docusign presents.

details: Choose `Multiple code examples, Authorization Code Grant and JWT Grant`.
Quickstart creates and configures the app and integration key.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The account must be owner or admin because the generator creates an app and integration key.

primary_path.action: Download and extract the ZIP, enter its directory, and install dependencies.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Resolve runtime or dependency errors before starting.

primary_path.output: The local launcher opens in a browser.
primary_path.success_signal: The Authorization Code Grant authentication flow begins.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Verify the personalized project was generated for the same sandbox account.

primary_path.success_signal: The first run proceeds to embedded signing.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Follow the displayed authorization recovery path.

primary_path.action: Verify that the main Quickstart opens the embedded-signing example.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Restart after correcting launcher or authorization errors.

branches.path: Choose Workflow, API Integration, Extension App, or Not Sure before submitting the account form.
branches.effect_on_first_success: May influence the hidden post-signup experience, but no public mapping is documented.

branches.condition: Generate the smaller Authorization Code Grant embedded-signing Quickstart
branches.path: Choose that project in the generator, download and extract the personalized ZIP, enter `quick_acg`, run `npm install` and `npm start`, open localhost, and authenticate through Authorization Code Grant to use its single embedded-signing workflow.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Narrows the project to authentication and one embedded-signing workflow instead of the selected main Quickstart.

branches.condition: Generate the smaller JWT Grant remote-signing Quickstart
branches.path: Choose that project in the generator, download and extract the personalized ZIP, enter `jwt_console_project`, run `npm install` and `npm start`, and use its single remote-signing workflow.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes authentication and the signing experience; first-time JWT impersonation consent may still be required.

branches.condition: Use the main Multiple code examples Quickstart
branches.path: Name the app, select Node.js and the main project, download and extract the personalized ZIP, run `npm install` and `npm start`, then authenticate with Authorization Code Grant and let the first run open embedded signing.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Follows the selected official main-project default and exposes broader examples only on later runs.

branches.condition: Configure the project manually instead of downloading a preconfigured Quickstart
branches.path: Create the app and integration key in Apps and Keys and populate the grant-specific settings and signer details yourself.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds app creation, credential generation, callback configuration, and secret-handling steps.

friction_gates.description: The developer-account form requires personal identity, company, country or region, and build-intent fields.
Get Started accepts Docusign's Terms and Conditions and Privacy Policy.

Verification, sandbox provisioning, and authenticated Quickstart access are hidden after form submission, and Quickstart requires the account owner or admin because it creates an app and integration key.
The generator requires an app name, language, and project-type choice; the overview identifies Multiple code examples as the main project and documents its first-run default.
Quickstart creates and preconfigures the selected route's app, integration key, account settings, and authentication code; manual credential setup applies only to the unconfigured launcher branch.
The selected main project first authenticates interactively with Authorization Code Grant; JWT alternatives can require first-time impersonation consent.
The selected Node.js Quickstart requires ZIP extraction, Node.js 8.10 or later, npm 5 or later, `npm install`, `npm start`, and a local browser session.

sources.title: eSignature REST API Quickstart | Docusign
evidence_supported: Node.js main and specialized project choices

sources.title: Docusign Node.js Launcher Code Examples
sections_used: Installation steps for JWT Grant authentication
JWT grant remote signing and Authorization Code Grant embedded signing projects
evidence_supported: Single remote and embedded signing workflows

sources.title: Quickstart overview | Docusign
evidence_supported: First-run Authorization Code Grant and embedded-signing default

excluded_after_success: Completing the embedded signature, confirming an envelope response, remote signing, polling envelope status, downloading completed documents, adding templates or recipients, production go-live review, switching from demo to production, webhook setup, and broader code examples occur after the main Quickstart opens its first embedded-signing example.
